rulings.Case Title: CHARLES EVANS JR. ET AL VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Asbestos Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 26, 2019, line 1. Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Leave to File Third Amended Complaint is DENIED with prejudice. Plaintiff failed to demonstrate an excusable delay in filing the proposed amendment. (Moss Estate Co. v. Adler (1953) 41 Cal.2d 581, 586; see also CRC 3.1324(b).) Plaintiff also has not shown that Defendant will not be prejudiced as discovery is closed and trial is set for January 13, 2020. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)

If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: CHICK DEE VS. INTERNATIONAL PAPER COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Asbestos Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 26, 2019, line 6. Defendant American Biltrite Inc.'s for summary judgment is DENIED. Defendant has not shifted the burden under Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 850. Plaintiff's deposition testimony regarding the number of times he worked on Amtico floor tile in New York versus California is insufficient for Defendant to meet its burden of showing that New York's interest would be more impaired if its policy were subordinated to the policy of California. (Adams Declaration, Ex. E at 235-36, 265-66, 272-73.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY CAMP ET AL VS. THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Substituting Successor-In-Interest For Deceased Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Asbestos Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 26, 2019, line 7. Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Substituting Successor-In-Interest for Deceased Plaintiff is GRANTED. Deanna Lee Kisling is substituted as successor-in-interest for deceased plaintiff, Jerry Camp pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 377.31. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)
